concentration.headline: Primate city dominance — one city overwhelms all others
concentration.body: Cairo accounts for a striking 35.1% of tracked urban population across 187 cities in Egypt. This level of primacy (35%) is characteristic of rapidly urbanising or historically centralised economies where one city concentrates political, economic and cultural power.

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Cairo & Giza
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Cairo and Giza (7 km apart, combined population 25,894,402). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 7 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Egypt exhibits strong primate city dominance: Cairo holds 35.1% of tracked urban population — a striking concentration. The gravity network confirms this: connections involving Cairo dominate, with Cairo → Giza among secondary links. This pattern is characteristic of historically centralised or rapidly urbanising economies.
